assumptions: The issue is assessed in the consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 hedge accounting.
The intragroup dividend creates foreign currency exposure either through a recognized monetary receivable or through related cash flows.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for a recognized asset or liability exposed to a particular risk affecting profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a separate top-level hedge accounting model and specifically addresses when intragroup foreign currency exposures can qualify in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for group foreign currency exposures.
